question: What is a hash table and what is its average-case lookup complexity?
expected_behavior: Textbook CS — define hash table + O(1) avg lookup, O(n) worst case.
ground_truth: A hash table is a key-value data structure that uses a hash function to compute an index into an array of buckets. Average-case lookup, insert, and delete are O(1); worst case is O(n) when collisions degrade performance.

answer_relevancy.error: Cannot copy out of meta tensor; no data! Please use torch.nn.Module.to_empty() instead of torch.nn.Module.to() when movi

pipeline.answer: A hash table is a data structure that stores key-value pairs and uses a hash function to compute an index into an array of buckets, enabling fast average-case O(1) lookup, insertion, and deletion. It resolves collisions via chaining or open addressing. The hash function distributes keys uniformly to achieve constant-time performance.
pipeline.rewritten_query: What is a hash table and what is its average-case lookup complexity?
pipeline.rationale: Textbook CS concept; well-established and timeless.
